question: What is the legal name of your company?
helperText: Provide the exact registered name of your company as per the documents.
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of your company as it appears on your GST registration, PAN card, or other official documents.
Avoid using abbreviations, acronyms, or informal names. The name should match your company's official registration.
If your company has multiple locations, use the name of the headquarters or primary place of business.

question: What is your business registration number?
helperText: This is the unique identification number assigned to your business by the government, such as your GST registration number or PAN (Permanent Account Number).
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Business Registration Number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 10-15 character alphanumeric business registration number as it appears on your official documents.
Common business registration numbers in India include the GST registration number (15 characters) and the PAN (Permanent Account Number, 10 characters).
Double-check your entry to avoid any typos or mistakes, as this information is crucial for your insurance policy.

question: What is your company's Goods and Services Tax (GST) registration number?
helperText: The GST number is a unique 15-digit identification number assigned to your business by the Indian government. It is mandatory for all businesses registered under the GST Act.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for providing your GST number
guidanceTips: Ensure you enter the exact 15-digit GST number as it appears on your GST registration certificate.
The GST number follows a specific format: XX-AAAAA-NNNN-Z-Y-Z, where XX is the state code, AAAAA is the PAN-based business entity number, NNNN is a unique number, and Z and Y are check digits.
If you have recently registered for GST, make sure to use the new GST number and not the old VAT/Service Tax registration number.

question: What is your company's Permanent Account Number (PAN)?
helperText: PAN is a unique 10-digit alphanumeric number issued by the Income Tax Department, India. It is mandatory for all businesses to have a valid PAN.
guidanceTips: The PAN number should be in the format: ABCDE1234X, where A-E are uppercase letters and 1234 are digits.
Ensure that the PAN number you provide is registered in the name of your business entity, not an individual.
Double-check the PAN number to avoid any typos or errors, as it is a critical identifier for your business.

question: What is the primary business type or industry of your organization?
helperText: Select the option that best describes your primary business activities.
guidanceTips: Choose the industry that most closely matches your organization's core business functions and revenue sources.
If your business operates in multiple industries, select the one that accounts for the majority of your revenue.
For diversified conglomerates, select the industry that best represents the largest business unit or revenue contributor.
If your business type is not listed, select 'Other' and provide a brief description in the next field.

question: What is the year your business was established?
helperText: Enter the 4-digit year your business was officially registered or started operations.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Year of Establishment
guidanceTips: Enter the exact 4-digit year your business was established, e.g. 2015.
If you're not sure of the exact year, provide your best estimate based on when your business started operations.
The year of establishment is important for insurance providers to understand the maturity and experience of your business.

question: What is the total number of employees in your organization?
placeholder: Enter the exact number of employees
helperText: Please provide the exact number of employees in your organization. This information is required to calculate the appropriate group health insurance premium.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing the Number of Employees
guidanceTips: The number of employees should include all full-time, part-time, and contractual workers in your organization.
Ensure that the number you provide is accurate and up-to-date, as it directly impacts the insurance premium calculation.
If your organization has seasonal or fluctuating employee numbers, provide the average number of employees over the past 12 months.

question: What is your company's total annual revenue?
placeholder: Enter your annual revenue in INR
helperText: Please provide your company's total revenue for the last financial year. This information is required to calculate the appropriate insurance coverage and premium.
guidanceTips: Enter the exact annual revenue figure, do not provide a range.
Ensure the revenue figure includes all sources of income for your business, including sales, services, and any other revenue streams.
If your company's fiscal year is different from the calendar year, provide the revenue for the most recently completed fiscal year.

question: What is the registered address of your business?
helperText: Provide the full address where your business operations are based, including the building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
guidanceTitle: Tips for Entering Your Business Address
guidanceTips: Make sure to include all relevant address details like building name, street, locality, city, state, and PIN code.
Ensure the address matches your company's registration documents like GST and PAN.
Provide the address where the majority of your business activities take place.

question: What is the city where your business is located?
helperText: Select the city where your business is located. This is required for accurate insurance coverage and premium calculation.
guidanceTips: Choose the city that matches the registered address of your business.
If your business has multiple locations, select the city where the majority of your employees are based.
Ensure the city name is spelled correctly and matches the legal documents of your business.

question: In which state is your business located?
options: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
helperText: Select the state where your business is registered and operating.
guidanceTips: Choose the state that matches the registered address of your business.
If your business operates in multiple states, select the state where your headquarters or primary office is located.
Ensure that the state selected matches the information provided in your GST registration and other official documents.

question: What is the PIN code of your business location?
helperText: The PIN code is a 6-digit postal code used in India to facilitate mail delivery. It is an important identifier for your business location.
guidanceTips: The PIN code must be a 6-digit number without any spaces or special characters.
Ensure you enter the PIN code for the exact location of your business, as this is used for various regulatory and insurance purposes.
If you are unsure of your business PIN code, you can look it up online using your city/town name or address.

question: Who is the primary contact person for this insurance policy?
placeholder: Enter full name of contact person
helperText: Provide the full name of the person who will be the primary point of contact for this insurance policy.
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the individual, including first, middle (if applicable), and last name.
Ensure the name matches the government-issued ID (such as PAN card or Aadhaar) that will be provided for this policy.
Avoid using abbreviations or nicknames in this field.

question: What is your official business email address?
helperText: Please provide the email address registered for your business. This will be used for all policy-related communication.
guidanceTips: Use the official email address associated with your registered business, not a personal email.
Ensure the email address is spelled correctly and does not contain any typos.
Avoid using generic email addresses like info@company.com or contact@company.com.

question: What is your business's primary contact phone number?
placeholder: Enter your 10-digit mobile or landline number
helperText: Please provide a valid 10-digit phone number where you can be reached. This will be used for policy-related communications.
guidanceTips: Enter your primary business contact number, which can be a mobile or landline.
The number should be 10 digits long and start with 6, 7, 8, or 9.
Ensure the number provided is active and regularly monitored, as it will be used for important policy updates and claims-related communications.

question: When would you like the group health insurance policy to start?
helperText: The policy period is typically 1 year. Please select a start date within the next 12 months.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Selecting the Policy Start Date
guidanceTips: The policy start date should be a date in the future, not the current date.
The start date should be within the next 12 months, as group health insurance policies are typically renewed annually.
Consider your business needs and the preferred timing for the policy to begin, such as the start of your company's fiscal year or a new quarter.

question: When does the group health insurance policy end?
helperText: Enter the date when the current group health insurance policy expires.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering the Policy End Date
guidanceTips: Group health insurance policies in India are typically issued for a period of 1 year.
The policy end date should be exactly 1 year after the policy start date.
Ensure the policy end date is within the valid range of 1 year from the current date.

question: What is the total coverage amount required for your group health insurance policy?
helperText: The coverage amount should be between ₹1,00,000 to ₹1,00,00,000 as per industry standards.
guidanceTips: Consider the total number of employees in your organization and their average health insurance needs.
Factor in any pre-existing conditions or specific coverage requirements for your employees.
Ensure the coverage amount is sufficient to meet the potential medical expenses of your entire group.
Review the coverage limits and sub-limits offered by different insurance providers to find the optimal plan.

question: What is the name of your current group health insurance provider?
placeholder: Enter the name of your current insurance provider
helperText: Provide the exact name of the insurance company that currently provides your group health coverage.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Entering Current Insurance Provider
guidanceTips: Enter the full legal name of the insurance company, not just a brand name or abbreviation.
If you have multiple group health policies, provide the name of the primary or largest provider.
Ensure the spelling and capitalization matches the official name on your current policy documents.

question: Please provide details of any previous health insurance claims made by you or your employees in the last 3 years.
helperText: Provide information on the nature of the claim, the amount claimed, and the outcome. This will help us assess the risk profile and provide an accurate quote.
guidanceTitle: Guidance for Providing Previous Claims History
guidanceTips: Be as detailed as possible in describing the previous claims, including the date, nature of the claim, amount claimed, and the outcome.
If there have been no previous claims, please mention 'No claims' in the field.
Providing accurate and complete information on previous claims will help us offer a tailored insurance solution that meets your needs.

question: Please describe the risk management measures your business has in place to mitigate potential health-related risks for your employees.
helperText: Provide details on your employee health and safety policies, wellness programs, emergency preparedness, and any other relevant measures.
guidanceTitle: Guidance on Describing Risk Management Measures
guidanceTips: Outline the specific steps your business takes to identify, assess, and mitigate health-related risks for your employees.
Highlight any health and safety protocols, training programs, or insurance coverages you have in place.
Explain how your risk management approach aligns with industry best practices and regulatory requirements in India.
